steps.title: Identify what the blank must do
steps.content: The blank precedes "methodology" and must be a possessive modifier. The antecedent is "the technique" (the Meisner technique) — a singular thing, not a person. The blank needs the singular possessive pronoun for a non-person antecedent.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each choice as the possessive before "methodology":
  • Choice A — they're: contraction of "they are" — violates two rules: contractions cannot function as possessives, and plural "they" does not agree with singular "the technique." ✗
  • Choice B — its: singular possessive pronoun for non-persons — correctly agrees with "the technique" and properly modifies "methodology." ✓
  • Choice C — it's: contraction of "it is" — reads as "it is methodology," which is ungrammatical; a contraction cannot serve as a possessive. ✗
  • Choice D — their: plural possessive pronoun — does not agree with singular "the technique"; number agreement is violated. ✗
That matches Choice B — its.
